title: Why Timothée Chalamet Lost Best Actor to Michael B. Jordan
text: According to the analysis, three factors are examined. NOT Due to Ballet/Opera Comments: The piece dismisses claims that Chalamet's controversial remarks about ballet and opera cost him the award. The backlash occurred after the March 5 voting deadline, and other actors have won Oscars despite more serious controversies. NOT Due to Playing an Unlikable Character: Playing a morally compromised protagonist didn't disqualify him. The article notes that past winners like Robert De Niro (Raging Bull) and Denzel Washington (Training Day) won for portraying terrible people. The Real Reasons: Youth, Brashness, and Jordan's Superior Performance. The article contends Chalamet's campaign misstep was adopting an arrogant persona while promoting Marty Supreme. The author notes he made embarrassing promotional choices, including a rap remix and boasted about delivering top-of-the-line performances. This self-aggrandizing approach—combined with his relative inexperience compared to legendary actors—likely hurt his candidacy. Ultimately, the piece concludes: Michael B. Jordan simply gave a better performance than Timmy this year.
